mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent recession, causing analysts to downgrade near-term earnings estimates and triggering a negative repricing of equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly GDP contraction and persistent month-over-month inflation erodes real corporate profit margins while forcing prolonged restrictive monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly negative when both GDP declines quarter-over-quarter and CPI remains above 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production in a post-shock recovery phase indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization improvement, which expands corporate operating leverage and drives positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 when INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% for two consecutive months, indicating overheating rather than normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 1.1 million units signals a sharp deceleration in residential investment and broader consumer confidence, triggering negative forward equity returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where HOUST drops below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll growth combined with stable federal funds rates indicates a soft landing scenario where earnings momentum outpaces discount rate hikes, producing positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative or flat when PAYEMS rises above 150,000 but FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer revolving credit balances reflect sustained household spending power and liquidity, which historically precedes positive corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation above 0.4% monthly forces market expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when CPI accelerates above the threshold but FEDFUNDS remains unchanged.
